title: duabanga moluccana
intro: Duabanga moluccana, also known as Daun Malam or Daun Jeruk Nipis, is a plant species native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines. The plant is a medium-sized tree or shrub with medicinal properties, primarily due to its leaves, bark, and roots, which contain compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids. The medicinal properties of Duabanga moluccana are attributed to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Duabanga moluccana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health benefits, including colds, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
